frameworks.name: drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: Decision variable as deterministic drift plus stochastic diffusion to a bound

frameworks.name: bounded evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Noisy evidence integrated over time until a commitment threshold is reached

frameworks.name: population coding
frameworks.one_line: Single scalar decision variable read out from weighted sum of LIP neurons

core_question: Can the drift-diffusion decision signal, including its stochastic component, be observed within a single trial in LIP population activity?
key_assumptions: Parietal and prefrontal neurons represent accumulated noisy evidence
A weighted sum across the population yields the decision variable
Stochastic diffusion confers variability in choice and decision time
manipulations: Random dot motion direction discrimination task
Simultaneous recording of hundreds of LIP neurons
Single-trial analysis rather than averaging across trials
key_findings: A single scalar from population activity carries both drift and diffusion on individual decisions
The stochastic diffusion component is recoverable within single trials, not only in trial-averaged activity
The recovered signal aligns with the quantity the brain uses to commit to a choice
interpretations: LIP population activity instantiates a single-trial drift-diffusion decision variable
Choice commitment is governed by this neurally instantiated accumulation signal
